title: Why Select Features?
concept: Selecting only the most relevant features prevents models from becoming slow, noisy, and prone to overfitting.

exercises.prompt: Warmup: Anomaly detection is the process of identifying unusual data points in a dataset. Which of the following is a classic example of anomaly detection?

options.text: Flagging a credit card transaction made in a different country minutes after a local one
Predicting tomorrow's temperature using a linear trendline
Sorting a list of user names in alphabetical order

exercises.explain: Exactly! Anomaly detection focuses on spotting outliers or rare events that deviate from the normal pattern. Now, let's explore how we handle the columns themselves.

cards.title: Packing too much luggage
cards.body: Imagine planning a weekend road trip. If you pack every single item in your house "just in case," your car slows down, wastes fuel, and runs out of room. Machine learning models face the exact same problem when we give them too many columns of data.

cards.title: The Curse of Dimensionality
cards.body: The Curse of Dimensionality refers to how models degrade as you add more features (columns). Having too many columns makes your dataset sparser, requiring exponentially more data to train a reliable model.

cards.title: Finding a friend in a maze
cards.body: Think of finding a friend in a single hallway (1D). Easy, right? Now, try finding them in a single-story building (2D), a multi-story hotel (3D), or a giant skyscraper (many dimensions). As dimensions grow, the search space explodes, making patterns much harder for the model to find.

cards.title: Noise in house pricing
cards.body: To predict house prices, we want [size, bedrooms, location]. If we also include the owner's favorite color, their cat's name, and the day of the week they bought the house, the model gets confused by this noise and makes worse predictions.

cards.title: More features isn't always better
cards.body: A common myth is that giving a model more features always makes it smarter. In reality, adding irrelevant columns adds noise, increases training time, and leads to overfitting — where the model memorizes useless details instead of learning the big picture.

cards.title: Feature bloat vs. selection
cards.body: Comparing a model with too many features versus one with selected features:

left.text:
Slow to train
Prone to noise & overfitting

right.text:
Fast to train
Learns robust, general patterns

exercises.prompt: What is the primary danger of training a model with too many irrelevant features?

options.text: The model will overfit to noise and perform poorly on new data
The model will automatically delete the extra features
The dataset will take up less storage space
The model's training speed will double

exercises.explain: Irrelevant features introduce noise, causing the model to learn coincidental patterns that do not generalize.

exercises.prompt: True or false: As you add more features to a model, the amount of data required to train it effectively stays the same.
exercises.explain: False! As dimensions increase, the data becomes sparser, requiring exponentially more rows to cover the search space.

exercises.sentence: The tendency of models to degrade, slow down, and require exponentially more data when given too many features is known as the curse of ___.
exercises.explain: The curse of dimensionality describes the mathematical difficulty of learning in high-dimensional spaces.

exercises.prompt: Which feature is most likely to be 'noise' when predicting whether a customer will click an ad?

options.text: The customer's favorite ice cream flavor
The time of day the ad is shown

exercises.explain: Ice cream flavor is highly unlikely to relate to ad clicking, making it a noisy, irrelevant feature.

exercises.prompt: You are building a model to predict student test scores. The dataset has 100 students but 500 features, including favorite movies, pet names, and childhood cities. What should you do first?

options.text: Select only the most relevant features, like study hours and past grades, before training
Train the model on all 500 features so it has the maximum possible information
Delete half the students to balance the dataset
Combine all 500 features into one long text string

exercises.explain: Exactly! Selecting a small set of high-signal features prevents overfitting, especially when you have very few rows (100 students) compared to features (500 columns). Next up: how to find and drop features that repeat the same information!
